_comment: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) Compliance Pack for UiPath
_description: NIST AI RMF 1.0 (January 2023) — four core functions: GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE. Maps UiPath platform settings to AI risk management categories and subcategories.
_usage: Version 1.0 — initial draft. Apply with: python3 uipath_governance.py --mode configure --pack nist_ai_rmf_policies.json --no-dry-run --jwt '...' --cookie '...'
_format: Each field is a rich object: { type, default, description, value, clause_ref, rationale, requirement, mapping_ids }. The 'value' key is what gets applied. All other keys are documentation.
_runtime_standards_comment: Standards evaluated at agent runtime by UiPathGovernanceMiddleware. NIST AI RMF provides AI-specific risk controls; OWASP Agentic covers the technical threat surface.

_requirement_legend.mandatory: MUST be set — NIST AI RMF identifies this as a core practice
_requirement_legend.conditional_mandatory: MUST be set when AI system is classified as high-impact
_requirement_legend.recommended: SHOULD be set — AI RMF best practice
_requirement_legend.optional: MAY be set — supplementary risk control

core_functions.GOVERN: Culture, processes, and people for AI risk management
core_functions.MAP: Context is established and risks identified
core_functions.MEASURE: Risks are analyzed, assessed, and tracked

NIST-AITrustLayer-v1._description: AI Trust Layer — platform-level controls for GOVERN and MEASURE functions

global-control-toggle.description: Master enable for AI Trust Layer governance controls.
global-control-toggle.clause_ref: GOVERN 1.1 — Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization
global-control-toggle.rationale: GOVERN 1.1 requires organizational policies for AI risk management. The master switch ensures all downstream controls activate consistently.

audit-logging.description: Enable comprehensive audit logging for all AI interactions.
audit-logging.clause_ref: GOVERN 1.4 — Organizational teams are committed to AI risk management; MEASURE 2.5 — AI risk and benefit data
audit-logging.rationale: MEASURE 2.5 requires maintaining AI risk and impact metrics. Audit logs are the primary evidence source for AI RMF accountability requirements.

prompt-audit-retention-days.description: Number of days to retain AI prompt and response audit logs.
prompt-audit-retention-days.clause_ref: MANAGE 4.2 — Mechanisms are in place to sustain risk management
prompt-audit-retention-days.rationale: MANAGE 4.2 requires sustained monitoring. 90 days provides sufficient history for trend analysis, incident investigation, and periodic AI RMF reviews.

agents.clause_ref: MAP 1.1 — Context is established for the AI lifecycle; GOVERN 2.1 — Roles and responsibilities for AI risk management
agents.rationale: MAP 1.1 requires documenting AI system context before deployment. Agents should be enabled only after risk categorization per AI RMF MAP function.

model-monitoring-enabled.description: Enable continuous monitoring of AI model performance and drift.
model-monitoring-enabled.clause_ref: MEASURE 2.7 — AI system performance is monitored; MANAGE 2.4 — Residual risks are monitored
model-monitoring-enabled.rationale: MEASURE 2.7 and MANAGE 2.4 require ongoing performance monitoring. Model drift and degradation are key AI risks requiring continuous measurement.

bias-detection-enabled.description: Enable automated bias and fairness monitoring for AI outputs.
bias-detection-enabled.clause_ref: MEASURE 2.11 — Fairness and bias in AI; GOVERN 5.1 — Organizational policies addressing AI bias
bias-detection-enabled.rationale: MEASURE 2.11 identifies bias as a core AI RMF measurement requirement. AI agents making automated decisions require bias monitoring to meet fairness characteristics.

impact-assessment-required.description: Require AI impact assessment before agent deployment.
impact-assessment-required.clause_ref: MAP 5.1 — Likelihood and impact of identified risks; MEASURE 2.5 — AI risk and benefit metrics
impact-assessment-required.rationale: MAP 5.1 requires risk likelihood and impact analysis before deployment. This gate enforces the assessment workflow before agents go to production.

risk-tier.description: AI system risk tier classification (low/standard/high/critical). Affects which controls activate.
risk-tier.clause_ref: MAP 1.5 — Organizational risk tolerances are determined; MAP 3.5 — Risk tiers for AI systems
risk-tier.rationale: MAP 1.5 and MAP 3.5 require risk tiering. Higher tiers trigger additional controls (human oversight, enhanced monitoring, audit frequency).

ai-disclosure-to-users.description: Require disclosure to end users when they are interacting with an AI system.
ai-disclosure-to-users.clause_ref: GOVERN 4.1 — Organizational teams document the basis for proceeding; MANAGE 4.1 — Response is monitored by appropriate teams
ai-disclosure-to-users.rationale: Transparency is a core NIST AI RMF trustworthy characteristic. Users must know when interacting with AI to maintain informed consent and appropriate trust calibration.

explainability-logging.description: Log reasoning chain and decision rationale for AI agent outputs.
explainability-logging.clause_ref: MEASURE 2.6 — AI system performance and effectiveness are evaluated; GOVERN 1.7 — Processes for accountability
explainability-logging.rationale: Explainability is a core NIST AI RMF characteristic. Decision chains must be reconstructable for post-incident analysis, audits, and regulatory queries.

NIST-Robot-v1._description: Robot execution settings — MANAGE and MAP function controls for runtime AI risk

human-in-loop-for-high-risk.description: Require human approval (Action Center) for AI actions classified as high-risk in the risk tier.
human-in-loop-for-high-risk.clause_ref: MANAGE 1.3 — Responses to mapped risks; GOVERN 6.1 — Policies for human oversight
human-in-loop-for-high-risk.rationale: MANAGE 1.3 requires appropriate responses proportional to risk. High-risk AI decisions must route through human oversight to maintain accountability and prevent automated harm.

action-center-enabled.description: Enable UiPath Action Center for human-AI collaborative decision workflows.
action-center-enabled.clause_ref: GOVERN 6.1 — Policies for human oversight of AI; MANAGE 1.3 — Risk response
action-center-enabled.rationale: Action Center is the UiPath implementation of human-in-the-loop oversight required by MANAGE function. Enables humans to review, approve, or override AI decisions.

max-tool-calls-per-session.description: Maximum number of external tool/API calls an agent may make per session.
max-tool-calls-per-session.clause_ref: MANAGE 2.2 — Risk tolerance thresholds; MAP 5.2 — Likelihood of risk occurrence
max-tool-calls-per-session.rationale: Unbounded agency is a key AI risk. Caps enforce minimum-necessary principle and reduce likelihood of cascading automation errors (MAP 5.2 likelihood control).

anomaly-detection-enabled.description: Enable runtime anomaly detection to flag unusual AI behaviour patterns.
anomaly-detection-enabled.clause_ref: MAP 5.1 — Likelihood and impact of identified risks; MEASURE 2.7 — AI system performance monitored
anomaly-detection-enabled.rationale: MAP 5.1 requires continuous risk identification. Runtime anomaly detection surfaces emerging AI risks that were not anticipated at design time.

context-window-logging.description: Log the full context window (redacted) passed to AI models for traceability.
context-window-logging.clause_ref: MEASURE 2.5 — AI risk and benefit data; GOVERN 1.4 — Accountability for AI risk
context-window-logging.rationale: Context logging supports root cause analysis after AI incidents but must be balanced against data minimisation. Disabled by default; enable only when incident investigation requires it.

NIST-Studio-v1._description: Studio development settings — GOVERN and MAP controls for AI development lifecycle

ai-use-documentation-required.description: Require developers to complete AI use case documentation before publishing automations using AI.
ai-use-documentation-required.clause_ref: GOVERN 1.2 — Accountability mechanisms for AI; MAP 1.1 — Context established for AI lifecycle
ai-use-documentation-required.rationale: MAP 1.1 requires documenting AI system context, intended purpose, and stakeholders. Development-time gate prevents undocumented AI use cases from reaching production.

model-card-required.description: Require model card / AI system card to be attached to published AI automations.
model-card-required.clause_ref: GOVERN 1.7 — Transparency documentation for AI; MAP 2.1 — Scientific findings documented
model-card-required.rationale: NIST AI RMF recommends AI system cards as accountability artifacts. Cards document capabilities, limitations, training data, and evaluation results — critical for responsible deployment.

allowed-ai-providers.description: Approved AI model providers for use in automations. Empty list = all allowed.
allowed-ai-providers.clause_ref: GOVERN 2.2 — Organizational risk management for AI third-party; MAP 5.2 — Third-party AI risk
allowed-ai-providers.rationale: GOVERN 2.2 requires managing third-party AI risk. Restricting to approved providers ensures procurement, legal, and security reviews have been completed for each provider.

_nist_rmf_mapping._comment: Maps UiPath governance controls (NM01-NM18) to NIST AI RMF categories and subcategories
